blog.title: GitHub Copilot Code Review Setup: Instructions, Firewall, and Runners

short.title: When the Workflow Cannot Be Predrawn

long.title: Ask, Collaborate, Explore, or Delegate to AI?

results.content:
Copilot code review has four control layers. Configure each one before auto-review.
https://indieseek.co/blogs/github-copilot-code-review-custom-instructions-setup/?utm_source=x&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-18&utm_content=blog #GitHub

GitHub Copilot code review now reads head-branch instructions and can use its own environment, firewall, and runner settings.\n\nThis guide turns those controls into a four-layer setup, a least-privilege workflow, and a controlled PR validation checklist. Use it before enabling automatic AI review across a repository.

Copilot code review now has four separate controls—and the risky part is assuming they all behave the same. This guide shows where instructions, dependencies, network access, and runners belong, plus how to validate the setup with one controlled PR.
https://indieseek.co/blogs/github-copilot-code-review-custom-instructions-setup/?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-18&utm_content=blog

Copilot code review is now a four-layer system, not one toggle. Map instructions, environment, firewall, and runner settings into a safer PR workflow.
https://indieseek.co/blogs/github-copilot-code-review-custom-instructions-setup/?utm_source=mastodon&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-18&utm_content=blog #GitHub #CodeReview

Copilot code review is not one switch. Instructions, environment, firewall, and runner each control a different risk. This guide maps them into one testable PR workflow.
https://indieseek.co/blogs/github-copilot-code-review-custom-instructions-setup/?utm_source=nostr&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-18&utm_content=blog

results.content:
You cannot pre-draw every agent workflow. See how orchestrator-workers discovers subtasks, delegates them, and checks one clear goal.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oUr1NKka3R8 #AIAgents

Some workflows can be planned step by step. Others reveal the required subtasks only after the work begins. This 65-second Tiny Agent shows how orchestrator-workers discovers, delegates, and synthesizes dynamic work while keeping one clear verification goal.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oUr1NKka3R8

What if you cannot know the subtasks before an agent starts? Orchestrator-workers lets a central model discover the work, delegate it, and synthesize the result. This 65-second explanation shows when that flexibility is worth the coordination cost.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oUr1NKka3R8

When subtasks only become clear after work begins, a fixed workflow breaks down. This 65-second Tiny Agent shows how orchestrator-workers keeps delegation tied to one checkable goal.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oUr1NKka3R8 #AIAgents #Automation

You cannot pre-draw every agent workflow. Orchestrator-workers discovers the required subtasks, delegates them, and synthesizes the result against one clear goal. See the pattern in 65 seconds.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oUr1NKka3R8

results.content:
More AI autonomy is not always better. Use this four-mode map to decide when to ask, collaborate, explore, or delegate—and when to switch.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S6cVeVgCaB0 #AIAgents

The best AI workflow is not always the most autonomous one. This video maps four work modes—asking, collaboration, exploration, and delegation—across human and agent intensity. Use the framework to choose a mode, set stopping conditions, and switch without losing outcome ownership.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S6cVeVgCaB0

Using AI the same way for every task creates either too much supervision or too much autonomy. This video gives you a four-mode map for asking, collaborating, exploring, and delegating—plus clear signals for when to switch.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S6cVeVgCaB0

More autonomy is not always the right move. Use this four-mode map to choose between asking, collaboration, exploration, and delegation—and switch modes with evidence.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S6cVeVgCaB0 #AIAgents #HumanAI

The right AI workflow depends on two things: how much human judgment the task needs and how much execution the agent can own. This four-mode map helps you ask, collaborate, explore, or delegate deliberately.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S6cVeVgCaB0
